description: A designed White House economic statistics graphic ('November Jobs Report'). The background is a full-bleed photograph of President Donald Trump in a navy blue suit and red tie, standing and clapping/applauding on a factory or steel-mill floor; behind him is a crowd of cheering supporters wearing orange high-visibility jackets and hard hats (steelworkers), with campaign signs partly reading 'AGE' and 'AMERICA', and large rolls of steel and yellow industrial machinery visible. The image is dark and dramatic with Trump spotlit at center. Across the top in an elegant white serif (Times-style) all-caps headline reads 'NOVEMBER JOBS REPORT'. Near the bottom a large bright green rectangle holds a huge white sans-serif statistic '+121,000', and beneath it white all-caps text reads 'PRIVATE JOB GROWTH SINCE SEPTEMBER / 100% AMONG AMERICANS, NOT ILLEGALS'. At the very bottom is the White House line-drawing emblem with 'The WHITE HOUSE' caption. The propaganda technique combines an authoritative official-looking data card with a heroic photo of the President among working-class Americans, framing job growth as nativist (jobs going to citizens 'not illegals'). Target audience: working-class and nationalist supporters, anti-immigration messaging tied to economic credit-claiming.
notable_text: 'NOVEMBER JOBS REPORT'; '+121,000'; 'PRIVATE JOB GROWTH SINCE SEPTEMBER 100% AMONG AMERICANS, NOT ILLEGALS'; 'The WHITE HOUSE'.
